items.title: Zero gradients before backward pass
items.avoid: Calling `.backward()` twice on the same graph without clearing `.grad` or using `zero_grad()`.
items.prefer: Call `optimizer.zero_grad()` (or `param.grad = None`) before each backward so gradients do not accumulate unintentionally.
items.why: PyTorch accumulates gradients by default; stale values add to new ones and corrupt updates.
items.example: Two backward passes on the same loss without zeroing doubles every `.grad` entry unless `retain_graph` and accumulation are intentional.

items.title: Verify custom gradients numerically
items.avoid: Shipping a hand-written backward without checking against finite differences.
items.prefer: Compare analytic ∇f to coordinate-wise central differences on random test points.
items.why: Sign errors and missing chain-rule terms are common in custom layers.
items.example: Assert `np.allclose(analytic_grad(x), numeric_grad(f, x), rtol=1e-5)` for several random x before training.

items.title: Match learning rate to gradient scale
items.avoid: Using a fixed η when ||∇L|| varies by orders of magnitude across layers or training.
items.prefer: Monitor gradient norms, use normalization (Adam, RMSprop) or gradient clipping when norms spike.
items.why: Local curvature and scale differ; raw ∇L magnitude alone does not set a safe step size.
items.example: If ||∇L|| jumps from 0.01 to 100 after a batch, η = 0.1 may destabilize unless clipped or adaptively scaled.
